top_weaknesses.issue: Lacks a concrete startup casualty / survival example beyond naming OpenAI, Anthropic, Google product launches; piece reads as consensus memo without proprietary observation
top_weaknesses.fix: Cut the a16z citation paragraph and replace with one specific named company (e.g. a wrapper that died after Operator, or a vertical app that survived) with a real before/after

top_weaknesses.issue: Voice flips between sharp platform-risk argument and consultant-abstraction lists ('Permissions. Audit logs. Workflow ownership. Data contracts. Review queues. Implementation playbooks.')
top_weaknesses.fix: Replace the bare-noun list with one or two specific examples (e.g. how Harvey's review queue saved a renewal)

top_weaknesses.issue: H2 'Defensibility Moves to the Boring Parts' is editorially memorable but vague for SEO/skim
top_weaknesses.fix: Sharpen to 'Where AI Startup Defensibility Actually Comes From' or name one boring part in the header

top_weaknesses.issue: Three-part-symmetry phrasing ('Not X but Y. Not X but Y. Not X but Y.') in 'Startups Are Moving Downstream' reads as AI-essay rhythm
top_weaknesses.fix: Keep one of the three; cut or vary the others

consensus_summary: Coherent platform-risk argument with the strongest pull-quote line of the batch ('It is a competitor with distribution.'). Held back from sharing by abstract supporting paragraphs and a missing concrete company casualty — strong enough to publish, not yet strong enough to spread.
